interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (JSON:API) — workspaces and state versions (stable)

relationships.current-state-version: data is {type: state-versions, id: sv-...} or null when the workspace has never stored state

attributes.hosted-state-download-url: URL for the raw state data (a temporary pre-signed archivist URL; on some installs it lives on the API host, on others on a separate archivist host)
attributes.hosted-json-state-download-url: URL for the stable external JSON form, may be absent
attributes.serial: integer — increases with every new state; the raw state document carries the same serial field
attributes.size: integer byte size of the raw state object when reported; optional metadata
attributes.checksum_note: the show response carries NO md5 or lineage attribute (an older internal note claimed it did; current official docs show neither), so integrity checking uses the optional size attribute plus the serial cross-check

hosted_state_download.auth_policy: the bearer token is attached ONLY when the download URL's origin is exactly the configured TFE API origin; any request to a different origin — including every redirect hop that leaves the API origin — must carry no Authorization header
hosted_state_download.redirects: the download may answer 302/307 with a Location header (archivist offload); follow at most 3 redirects manually, re-applying the auth policy per hop; more than 3 is an error

readiness.rule: only a state version with status finalized may be downloaded; pending or discarded is a not-ready error and no download request is made

integrity.size_rule: when the size attribute is present, the downloaded byte count must equal it exactly; when absent, the size check is skipped
integrity.serial_rule: the downloaded raw state parses as JSON whose serial field must equal the state version's serial attribute
integrity.failure_rule: on any integrity failure nothing is written: the destination file is not created, an existing destination file is left byte-identical, and no temporary file remains

atomic_write.rule: the verified bytes are written to a temporary file in the destination directory and renamed into place; after success only the destination file exists (no temp siblings)

errors.client_rule: every non-2xx /api/v2 response decodes into a structured API error carrying the HTTP status code and the parsed errors array
errors.not_found_masking: 404 Not Found is returned both for resources that do not exist and for resources the token is not authorized to see
